title: A Korean trio of restaurants heads to Allston, starting with Sanbada
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Sanbada, the first of a Korean restaurant trio, is now open in Allston at 165 Brighton Ave.
Bombbada, offering night dining until 2 a.m., is set to open in July next to Sanbada.
Jinjee, a Korean BBQ spot part of the trio, is expected to open in mid-April in the same location.

title: The 5 most exciting books to read this spring — what’s on your list?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Rosie Hewlett's 'Sweetbitter Song' offers a historical retelling of the 'Odyssey' focusing on a sapphic love story.
Tara Menon's 'Under Water' explores friendship and loss against the backdrop of disasters, releasing on March 17, 2026.
Caro Claire Burke's 'Yesteryear' is a psychological thriller about a modern influencer trapped in 1855, out April 7, 2026.

title: Vermont Green FC upset Portland Hearts of Pine in regional rivalry U.S. Open Cup clash
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Vermont Green FC defeated Portland Hearts of Pine 1-0 in the U.S. Open Cup's first-ever matchup on Tuesday.
David Ismail scored the decisive goal in the 40th minute for Vermont Green FC in front of a sold-out home crowd.
Vermont Green FC, a USL League Two team, achieved an upset victory over higher-tier USL League One side Portland.

title: Police arrest 15-year-old in East Boston stabbing after he allegedly returns to the crime scene for his cellphone
author: John Waller
bullets: Police arrested a 15-year-old in East Boston after he returned to retrieve a cellphone at a stabbing crime scene.
The victim, who refused to give up his belongings, including a scooter, was stabbed but sustained non-life-threatening injuries.
The juvenile suspect is charged with aggravated assault and armed robbery; officers found knives and a ski mask nearby.

title: Woman, 45, killed in Newton crash
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 45-year-old woman died after a two-car crash in Newton near Walnut Street and Judith Road.
Her vehicle struck a tree following a collision with another car; she was pronounced dead at the hospital.
The second driver involved did not suffer serious injuries; the crash remains under investigation by authorities.

title: 1 dead after car crashes into tree in Chelmsford
author: John Waller
bullets: A man died after a car crash into a tree on Littleton Road in Chelmsford at 12:30 p.m. Tuesday.
The driver, suffering serious injuries from the crash, was taken to a hospital and pronounced dead.
Authorities have not yet publicly identified the deceased, and the crash is under investigation.

title: Local obituary: Janet Swallow, 68, Danvers resident and longtime Lahey nurse
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: Janet M. Swallow, 68, a longtime nurse at Lahey Medical Center, died on March 12, 2026, in Danvers.
Born in New York, she earned a nursing degree from Boston College and worked at hospitals in Danvers and Burlington.
She is survived by her children, grandchildren, siblings, and friends; visiting hours will be held on March 22, 2026.

title: A decade after her father’s death, 24-year-old runs the Boston Marathon for grief support
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Violet Morgan, 24, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for The Children's Room, honoring her late father.
Her father, John Morgan, passed away in 2016 from colorectal cancer, inspiring her participation in the marathon.
The Children's Room provides free grief support services for families experiencing the death of a parent or sibling.

title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (Mar. 18)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: Abington home on Rice St., built in 1951, 704 sq ft, sold for $460,000.
Amesbury's Orchard St. Colonial, 1,536 sq ft, built in 1991, sold for $701,050.
Boston condo on Beacon St., built in 1899, 5,703 sq ft, sold for $17,000,000.

title: 3 things the Bruins must fix if they want to punch their ticket to the playoffs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins' power play dropped from third to 28th in the NHL, scoring only 12.8% since the Olympic break.
Morgan Geekie and Elias Lindholm have zero 5-on-5 points in the last 11 games, hurting Bruins' offensive depth.
The third defensive pairing struggles, with Andrew Peeke and Nikita Zadorov outscored 4-2 in recent 5-on-5 play.
